justification: A correctly places Leidos (job 4) at 38 — well below the good-fit cluster — while B gives it 65, failing to adequately penalize a defense/clearance role for a web/AI candidate. A also correctly scores Snyk (job 3) at 72 vs good-fit jobs at 85-88, creating meaningful separation, whereas B's spread is compressed (65-92) with Leidos too high. A's skill_gap_accuracy is superior: it lists no fabricated gaps for strong matches and correctly identifies security tooling gaps for Snyk. B fabricates gaps like 'Canadian work authorization' for Magna (job 5), 'UK work authorization' for Snyk (job 3), 'Enterprise AI governance' and 'Model fine-tuning' for LegalZoom AI (job 8), and 'Traditional ML frameworks beyond LLMs' for a strong AI match (job 1) — these are either irrelevant or not genuine skill gaps. B's reasoning is also more generic in places ('Solid general SWE fit', 'Good general SWE fit') while A consistently references specific candidate artifacts like 'internshipmatcher.com', 'Burnt (YC S25)', and concrete metrics.
